clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall photograph the products described in [PRODUCT_LIST] at [SHOOT_LOCATION] on [SHOOT_DATE].

The Service Provider shall exercise reasonable care in handling the products described in [PRODUCT_LIST] while in its possession. The Client shall bear the risk of loss or damage to the products except to the extent caused by the Service Provider's negligence.
The Service Provider shall return the products described in [PRODUCT_LIST] to the Client within [PRODUCT_RETURN_PERIOD_DAYS] days of completing the Services.
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [SHOOT_DATE] at [SHOOT_LOCATION], subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.

The Client shall pay the Service Provider [PRICE_PER_PRODUCT] per product photographed, for an estimated total of [TOTAL_VALUE] based on [ESTIMATED_PRODUCT_COUNT] products, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD]. The final invoice shall reflect the actual number of products photographed.

Upon full payment, the Client receives a license to use the Deliverables for e-commerce listings, advertising, and marketing of the products described in [PRODUCT_LIST]. The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — per-unit pricing explicit with both rate and estimated count as separate Dynamic Fields, resolving Step 2 risk #3. Duplication: Product Handling (care/risk during shoot) vs Commercial Usage (post-shoot rights) address entirely distinct phases, no overlap. Loophole closed: added explicit return timeline, absent from initial draft, closing a gap where physical goods could be held indefinitely. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: risk-of-loss allocation explicitly favors Client except for photographer negligence, consistent with general liability carve-out pattern used market-wide. Complexity: minimal, no Image Rights Block needed since no identifiable persons are typically involved (unlike Wedding/Portrait/Fashion). Enforceability: care standard tied to "reasonable care" paired with an explicit negligence-based exception, satisfying PLDS-001's anchoring requirement for qualifiers. Attack: Product is damaged during the shoot and photographer denies responsibility — mitigated: risk allocation explicitly shifts to photographer where damage results from negligence. Attack: Client is billed for far more products than agreed without prior estimate — mitigated: per-unit clause anchors to an estimated count while final invoice reflects actual count transparently. Attack: Photographer retains products indefinitely awaiting payment — mitigated by explicit Return Timeline Clause operating independently of payment disputes (which are addressed via general Payment Block suspension patterns market-wide). No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
